FROM: marco.ferretti@voltcell-eu.com
TO: supply.chain@acmemotors.com

SUBJECT: EV Battery Packs – New Proposal from VoltCell

Good morning,

Marco Ferretti here from VoltCell, we spoke briefly at the Munich Motor Show in March.
I wanted to follow up with a concrete offer because I think there's a real fit between what
we produce and what your EV platform needs going into 2027 model year production.

We manufacture LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate) battery packs in two configurations —
a 62 kWh mid-range pack and a 88 kWh long-range pack.
Both are designed to the standard 800V architecture and fit a skateboard platform layout.
Thermal management is liquid-cooled and the packs carry a 10-year/300,000 km performance warranty.

Pricing — the 62 kWh unit is €7,200 per pack and the 88 kWh is
€9,850 per pack at volumes of 2,000+ units per order. Below 2,000 we'd add
roughly 8% to both. These prices are CIF your plant gate and include all cabling harnesses
and BMS pre-installed.

For an initial partnership order I'd suggest something like 1,500 units of the 62 kWh
and 800 units of the 88 kWh to start — that gives you flexibility across
trims without committing the full volume upfront. Lead time from confirmed PO is
14 weeks, so if you're targeting a January 2027 SOP you'd need to lock this
in by early October at the latest.

Delivery would be to your assembly facility — I don't have the exact address on file, could
you send that over? We typically ship on dedicated flatbed logistics from our Leipzig plant and
we coordinate directly with the receiving dock to handle the packs safely (they're Class 9
hazmat so there's paperwork involved, but we manage all of that on our end).

I haven't included crash test certifications in this email but we're fully UN38.3 and ECE R100
compliant — happy to send the full dossier.

Let me know if you want to set up a technical review call with your engineering team.
I can bring our applications engineer along.

Best,
Marco Ferretti
Key Account Manager – Automotive | VoltCell Europe

SUBJECT: OEM TWS Earbuds Proposal – SoniCraft HK (Updated Pricing May 2026)

Hi there!

I'm Jenny from SoniCraft HK — we do OEM/ODM for wireless audio products and we've been
making earbuds for brands in Europe and North America for about 6 years now. Kevin CC'd here
handles the technical side if there are any spec questions.

We have a new model we're really excited about — internally we call it the SC-A7 but obviously
if you white-label it you'd use your own name. It's a true wireless stereo earbud
with ANC (active noise cancellation), 10mm dynamic driver, Bluetooth 5.4, and about
8 hours battery in the bud + 32 hours in the case. IPX5 rated.
The case does wireless charging which is pretty much a must-have now at this price point.

Pricing depends on volume obviously. For 10,000 to 30,000 pairs we can do
$14.80 per pair (this includes the charging case, USB-C cable, two sizes of
silicone tips, and the retail box if you want us to do custom packaging — add $0.60 per unit
for that). Above 30,000 we'd go to $13.20 per pair. These are FOB Shenzhen prices.

Minimum order is 5,000 pairs. Tooling for custom shell colour is a one-time fee of $3,500 per
colour — we have 6 standard colours available with no tooling charge (white, black, navy, sage green,
coral, slate grey).

Production lead time — if you place the order within the next 3 weeks we can hit a
July 15 shipment date from Shenzhen port. After that it'll push to August.
We ship DDP or FOB depending on your preference. Most of our US and EU clients prefer FOB and
handle their own freight forwarder.

I should mention — we just passed the FCC and CE certification last month for the SC-A7,
so the compliance documentation is fresh and ready to go. We can also support EN 50566 for
SAR if you need it for certain EU markets.

Can I send you a sample unit? We can get two pairs to your address within a week via DHL.
Just send me a shipping address and I'll get that arranged at no charge.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

Best,
Jenny Wu
Sales Manager | SoniCraft HK Ltd

FROM: alex.park@koreavision.co.kr
TO: product.partnerships@acmemotors.com
CC: tech@koreavision.co.kr
DATE: Mon, 18 May 2026 09:30:00 +0900
SUBJECT: OEM Integrated Dashcam Module Proposal – KoreaVision Systems

Dear ACME Motors team,

My name is Alex Park from KoreaVision Systems, Seoul. We are a Tier-2 automotive supplier
specialising in integrated camera and vision modules. I believe you are currently evaluating
suppliers for your 2028 model year connected vehicle platform, and I'd like to put our
KV-DC900 dashcam module on the table for consideration.

Quick overview of the KV-DC900 — it's an integrated front + rear dual-channel dashcam
designed for OEM factory installation (not aftermarket). Front camera is 4K/30fps with HDR,
rear is 1080p/60fps. It connects to the vehicle's CAN bus and integrates with the infotainment
head unit over LVDS. We have reference designs for Android Automotive OS and QNX if either
of those is your platform. Parking mode works off the OBD-II hardwire without a separate battery pack.

Pricing — at 10,000 units per year we're looking at $68 per module
(complete system — front cam, rear cam, main processing unit, all wiring harness, mounting brackets).
At 25,000 units/year that comes down to $57 per module.
There's a one-time NRE (non-recurring engineering) fee of $45,000 for vehicle-specific
integration and validation work — this covers up to 2 vehicle platforms. If you have a third platform
that's an additional $18,000.

Delivery and capacity — our standard annual capacity is 80,000 units from our Incheon facility.
We do not single-source any critical component so supply chain risk is low. First production
units for validation would be available 16 weeks after NRE kickoff, and SOP
for production volumes would follow approximately 6 weeks after that.

I should flag that the KV-DC900 has already completed ADAS hardware compatibility testing
with Mobileye SuperVision, but we have not yet done NCAP-specific testing as a standalone product —
we typically do that jointly with the OEM during the integration phase. If ACME has specific
NCAP requirements let's talk about that separately, it affects the NRE timeline.

We're planning to be in Detroit June 9–11 for meetings — would love to do a live demo at your
office if the timing works. Our tech lead can bring a reference vehicle with the full system installed.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Warm regards,
Alex Park
Head of Global Sales | KoreaVision Systems
alex.park@koreavision.co.kr | +82-10-3847-2291

FROM: petra.novak@autobot-robotics.cz
TO: operations.procurement@acmemfg.com
CC: jan.horak@autobot-robotics.cz
DATE: Tue, 26 May 2026 10:00:00 +0200
SUBJECT: Commercial Proposal – AutoBot AR-6 Welding Robot Systems (ACME Manufacturing)

Dear ACME Manufacturing Procurement Team,

Following our productive meeting at the Hannover Messe exhibition last week, I am pleased to
submit a formal commercial proposal for the supply of AutoBot AR-6 six-axis welding robot
systems for your new production line in Brno.

The AR-6 is a 6-axis articulated robot arm with a 10 kg payload, 1,650mm reach,
and ±0.02mm repeatability. It ships complete with the AutoBot WeldMaster controller, teach pendant,
MIG/MAG welding torch package (Fronius CMT-compatible), and our standard safety fencing kit for a
single cell. Installation, commissioning, and 5-day operator training are included in the price below.

Based on our discussions, you indicated a requirement for 12 units for Phase 1
of the line. Our pricing for this configuration:

Unit price (12 units): €84,500 per robot system (inclusive of all above)
Phase 1 total: €1,014,000
Optional extended warranty (Year 3–5): €4,200/unit/year
Optional remote monitoring subscription (AutoBot Connect): €1,800/unit/year

If ACME proceeds with Phase 2 (which we understand could be another 8 units in early 2027),
we can lock in the same per-unit price for 18 months from the date of the Phase 1 PO — this
protects you from any material cost increases on our side.

Delivery and implementation schedule — our current production slot availability allows for
an 18-week lead time from signed contract and 30% deposit. This would place
delivery at your Brno facility in late October 2026, which aligns with the
Q4 line readiness date you mentioned. Commissioning would take approximately 3 weeks on-site
with a team of two AutoBot engineers.

One point raised during our Hannover discussion — you asked about compatibility with your
existing Siemens SINUMERIK line controller. Jan, our applications engineer (CC'd), has confirmed
that the AR-6 supports PROFINET integration natively and can receive weld path programs directly
from your existing CAM environment. He's available for a technical call at short notice if your
engineering team wants to verify this before the proposal goes to finance.

We require a Letter of Intent by June 20, 2026 to hold the production slot.
After that date, the Q4 slot cannot be guaranteed.

I'll follow up by phone Thursday morning if I don't hear from you first.

Yours sincerely,
Petra Novak
Regional Sales Director – Central Europe | AutoBot Robotics
petra.novak@autobot-robotics.cz | +420 777 334 820

Sitzwerk GmbH has been producing complete seat systems for OEM customers for 22 years.

email_20:
FROM: arjun.menon@sensnode.io
TO: r&d.procurement@acmecorp.com
DATE: Fri, 23 May 2026 17:30:00 +0530
SUBJECT: Industrial IoT Sensor Modules – SensNode Partnership Proposal

Hi there,

Arjun from SensNode here — we're a hardware startup out of Kochi, about 3 years old, and we
build industrial-grade IoT sensor modules for manufacturing and logistics environments. I came across
ACME Corp at the Embedded World India conference and one of your engineers (I think her name was Divya?)
mentioned you're actively looking at smart factory instrumentation for your new line. That's exactly
what we do so I thought I'd reach out directly.

Our flagship product right now is the SN-ENV300 — it's a multi-parameter
environment sensor module that reads temperature, humidity, ambient light, vibration (3-axis accelerometer),
and air quality (VOC + CO2 equivalent) all in one unit. It communicates over LoRaWAN, Wi-Fi, or
RS-485 Modbus depending on which variant you need, and it runs for up to 3 years on two
AA batteries in LoRa mode which is the big thing for factory floor deployments where running
power cables is a pain.

Pricing — I'll be straight with you since I know procurement people don't like dancing around it:

LoRaWAN variant: ₹6,400/unit (~$77) for 100+ units, drops to ₹5,800
(~$70) for 500+ units
Wi-Fi variant: ₹5,200/unit (~$62) for 100+ units, ₹4,700 (~$56) for 500+
RS-485 Modbus: ₹4,900/unit — this one's the simplest, most reliable for fixed
installations where you have existing fieldbus wiring

We also have a gateway unit (SN-GW100) for the LoRa variant if you don't already have LoRa
infrastructure — that's ₹18,500 per gateway, covers about 200 sensors in a clear factory environment
(less if there's a lot of metal racking, which I know manufacturing floors usually have).

A typical factory floor deployment for a medium-sized line would be something like 80–120 sensor
nodes and 2–3 gateways. I can do a proper site assessment and recommend exact quantities if you
give me a floor plan or let me visit — I'm happy to come to your facility, no charge for that obviously.

Delivery — if you order before June 15 I can ship by July 20 from our
Kochi facility. We're a small team so I want to be transparent — anything above 300 units needs
8 weeks lead time from our end. We don't carry large finished goods inventory but our component
supply is solid (all Mouser/Digi-Key sourced, no grey market parts).

One thing I want to flag — we haven't yet completed ATEX certification for explosive atmospheres.
If any part of your facility is Zone 1 or Zone 2 classified, we're not the right fit for those
areas right now. General factory floor with standard ventilation — totally fine, we have CE and
FCC and a BIS registration in progress.

Would love to get on a call this week or next. Happy to do a 30-minute product demo over video
first if you want a quick look before committing time for a site visit.

Cheers,
Arjun Menon
Co-founder & Head of Sales | SensNode Technologies
arjun.menon@sensnode.io | +91-94474-88201
